metadata.description: Thin skill that wraps the official Obsidian v1.12+ CLI for vault-aware file creation, search, navigation, task toggling, and tag inspection. Non-authoritative mirror foundation for future elegy-planning obsidian planning-mirror commands.
metadata.summary: Drive the user's installed obsidian CLI from agent contexts with deterministic capability boundaries and a uniform result envelope.
metadata.author: Elegy Contributors

capabilities.name: List Vaults
capabilities.description: List all Obsidian vaults registered with the desktop app.

output.description: Vault list result with raw multi-line text; each line is one registered vault.

capabilities.name: Read File
capabilities.description: Read the contents of a note by its path inside the active vault.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Vault-relative path of the note to read (e.g. 'notes/foo.md' or 'daily/2026-06-02.md').

output.description: File contents; data contains the note text and rawOutput mirrors it for callers that want one source of truth.

capabilities.name: Create File
capabilities.description: Create a new note in the active vault. Fails if the note already exists.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Vault-relative path of the note to create (e.g. 'notes/foo.md').

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Optional initial file content. Pass --content or pipe via stdin if the note is large.

output.description: Creation confirmation; status ok when the file was created.

capabilities.name: Append To File
capabilities.description: Append text to the end of a note without rewriting the existing content.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Vault-relative path of the note to append to.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Text to append.

capabilities.name: Patch File
capabilities.description: Apply a targeted patch to a note: insert, replace, or delete at a specific line range.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Vault-relative path of the note to patch.

parameters.name: start_line
parameters.description: Inclusive start line for the patch window (1-based).

parameters.name: end_line
parameters.description: Inclusive end line for the patch window. Use the same value as start_line to target a single line.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Patch mode: 'replace' (default), 'insert-before', 'insert-after', or 'delete'.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Replacement or inserted content. Ignored when mode is 'delete'.

capabilities.name: Move Or Rename File
capabilities.description: Move or rename a note within the active vault, preserving Obsidian's wiki link rewrites when possible.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Source vault-relative path.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Destination vault-relative path.

capabilities.name: Delete File
capabilities.description: Delete a note from the active vault. Destructive; agents must confirm scope before invoking.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Vault-relative path of the note to delete.

capabilities.name: Search Notes
capabilities.description: Search the active vault for a free-text query and return matching file paths with optional context lines.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search query string.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Optional vault-relative path scope for the search.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Optional maximum number of matches to return.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Optional output format: 'text' (default) or 'json' for structured results when supported by the installed CLI build.

output.description: Search hits. data is an array of file paths (and context when format=json); rawOutput mirrors text format.

capabilities.name: Read Daily Note
capabilities.description: Read today's daily note (or the daily note for a specific date) from the active vault.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Optional ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD). Omit for today's daily note.

capabilities.name: Append To Daily Note
capabilities.description: Append text to today's daily note (or the daily note for a specific date) in the active vault.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Optional ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD). Omit for today's daily note.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Text to append.

capabilities.name: Random Note
capabilities.description: Open or print a random note from the active vault.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Optional vault-relative path scope to sample from.

output.description: Randomly selected note path and contents.

capabilities.name: List Tags
capabilities.description: List all tags used across the active vault, with occurrence counts when the CLI supports them.

output.description: Tag list (one per line, often prefixed with counts).

capabilities.name: List Notes For Tag
capabilities.description: List all notes in the active vault that carry a specific tag.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Tag name to filter by, without the leading '#'.

output.description: File paths of notes containing the tag.

capabilities.name: List Tasks
capabilities.description: List tasks across the active vault. Filter by done/undone state and optional scope.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Optional filter: 'done', 'undone', or 'all' (default).

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Optional vault-relative path scope.

output.description: Task list (one per line, including file:line provenance when supported).

capabilities.name: Toggle Task
capabilities.description: Toggle the done state of a specific task identified by its file and line number.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Vault-relative path of the note containing the task.

parameters.name: line
parameters.description: 1-based line number of the task bullet.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Target state: 'done' or 'undone'. Omit to toggle the current state.

capabilities.name: Run Obsidian Command
capabilities.description: Invoke a registered Obsidian command by its command id (e.g. 'app:reload', 'workspace:close').

parameters.name: command_id
parameters.description: Obsidian command identifier.

output.description: Command execution confirmation; data carries the resolved command id.

capabilities.name: Obsidian CLI Version
capabilities.description: Report the installed obsidian CLI version. Use as a precondition check before capabilities that need a minimum CLI build.

output.description: Version string; rawOutput carries the raw version line.

constraints.description: All capabilities shell out to the official Obsidian v1.12+ CLI that ships with the user's installed Obsidian desktop application. The CLI must be enabled in Obsidian Desktop settings (Settings -> General -> Command line interface) and the 'obsidian' binary must be on PATH.

This skill is a non-canonical vault interface. It must never be the source of truth for plan/roadmap/goal state. SQLite via elegy-planning remains the durable authority. Obsidian content is a read mirror or a write surface for the user's own notes; durable planning data must continue to flow through elegy-planning and the SQLite database.

Capabilities must not create, update, or delete files that look like elegy-planning projections (e.g. notes inside '.copilot/backlogs' or any path that would shadow planning authority). Use a dedicated mirror folder, typically 'inbox/', 'notes/', or 'planning-mirror/', scoped to the user's vault policy.

triggers.description: When an agent needs to discover or operate against the user's Obsidian vault through the official CLI.
When the user asks to create a new note in their vault.
When the user asks to search inside their vault.
When the user wants to log an entry in today's daily note.
When the user wants to mark a checkbox-style task done or undone in a vault note.
